headline: Unit (HyperUnit) is an off-chain Guardian-Network-based asset bridge layer for Hyperliquid; specific smart contracts on source chains not yet enumerated
short_headline: Guardian-based bridge; contracts to be enumerated

findings.text: Unit protocol is an off-chain Guardian Network infrastructure, not a collection of named smart contracts on block explorers. No tagged contracts found on Etherscan or other explorers under 'Unit' or 'HyperUnit' name searches. The protocol operates as distributed nodes generating deposit/withdrawal addresses per-user.

Official deployment documentation at https://docs.hyperunit.xyz/developers/key-addresses/mainnet contains Guardian public keys and protocol component references but developer API pages are access-restricted. No traditional 'Deployed Contracts' table with addresses available in browsable docs.

No audit links provided in pinned protocol metadata. No audit PDFs discovered in searches or document walks. Protocol is maintained by Unit Labs and claims self-funding with expert team from HRT, Jump, Fortress.

No GitHub repository recorded in pinned metadata. Search for 'Unit protocol site:github.com' returned an older 'unitprotocol/core' (stablecoin minter, not bridge) and no HyperUnit/Unit bridge source repo was surfaced. The protocol appears to be closed-source or private.

Protocol claims support across Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, Plasma, Monad (pinned); additional assets ENA documented. Without enumerable smart contracts on each chain, only Guardian Network operator infrastructure catalogued, not per-chain contract addresses.
No factory contracts identified. Unit does not follow a factory-spawned-children pattern; Guardian Network dynamically generates deposit/withdrawal addresses per-user via consensus.

Guardian Network operators and Unit Labs are the administrative actors. Guardian public keys published (e.g., 04dc6f89... and 048633ea...) but exact on-chain admin/upgrade/pause contracts not identified. Role classification requires on-chain contract enumeration.

All evidence for Unit's existence and architecture sourced from official docs (hyperunit.xyz), third-party research (newsletter.asxn.xyz, blocmates.com, defillama.com), and regulatory compliance page. No block-explorer or on-chain evidence yet collected due to architecture being primarily off-chain relay/consensus.

rationale.verdict: Unit (HyperUnit) is an off-chain bridge protocol built on a Guardian Network consensus architecture rather than traditional smart contracts. The pinned address_book is null, and searches/fetches did not surface enumerable contract addresses on target chains (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, Plasma, Monad). The protocol maintains deposit addresses dynamically via guardian consensus. Without access to restricted developer API docs or a public GitHub source repo, specific smart contract addresses on each chain cannot be catalogued. Downstream evaluation slices will require either direct enumeration via block explorers or access to the restricted docs pages. Set grade=unknown pending contract discovery.

evidence.shows: Unit is the asset tokenization layer on Hyperliquid, enabling deposits and withdrawals of BTC, ETH, SOL across Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana chains
Detailed protocol architecture including Guardian Network, Agent system, 2-of-3 MPC TSS consensus, lock-and-mint mechanism for cross-chain asset movement
Guardian Network comprises distributed operators running Agent protocol software; frontend at app.hyperunit.xyz maintained by Unit core team
Guardian public keys published for address verification; mainnet infrastructure documentation exists but API pages access-restricted
2-of-3 MPC threshold signature scheme, deterministic state machine, secure enclaves for key storage, compliance screening (OFAC, VPN detection)
Unit Protocol is noncustodial, decentralized; maintained by independent Guardian validators; Unit Labs operates frontend at app.hyperunit.xyz; legal contact: legal@hyperunit.xyz
Team expertise from HRT, Jump, Fortress organizations; protocol focused on on-chain verification and resilience; launched native BTC Feb 14, 2025; extended to ETH, SOL
Unit categorized as asset bridge; TVL tracked via HyperUnit hot wallets for BTC/ETH/SOL; GitHub code reference available but URL not directly accessible
Guardian Network uses 2-of-3 MPC TSS requiring signature from majority of Guardians; April 15, 2025 service disruption reported; team transparency concerns noted

unknowns: D1-eth: Etherscan name-tag search for 'Unit' or 'HyperUnit' did not surface contract addresses; bridge contracts may be unlabeled or deployed under different names
D1-btc: Bitcoin/Plasma/Monad/Solana block explorer searches not attempted due to tool limitations; these chains may have enumerable contract/wallet addresses for asset custody
D2: Developer API docs at https://docs.hyperunit.xyz/developers/api.md are access-restricted; no public 'Deployed Contracts' reference page accessible
D3: No audit links in pinned metadata; no audit reports discovered in public searches or docs; security posture unknown
D4: No GitHub source repository found; protocol appears closed-source or not publicly versioned; unable to pin code commits

D5-offchain: Protocol architecture is primarily off-chain (Guardian Network consensus); traditional per-chain contract enumeration may not apply; Plasma/Monad/Bitcoin lock-and-mint contracts not yet identified
D6: Unit generates deposit addresses dynamically; no factory contracts identified; address enumeration not applicable
D7: Guardian public keys documented but corresponding on-chain admin/upgrade authorities not mapped; multisig or governance contract structure unknown
D8: Block-explorer evidence required to complete address catalog; downstream slices will need either exposed dev docs or direct chain searches

protocol_metadata.about: Unit is the asset tokenization layer on Hyperliquid, enabling seamless cross-chain deposits and withdrawals of major cryptocurrencies (BTC, ETH, SOL, and others) between their native blockchains and the Hyperliquid exchange. The protocol employs a Guardian Network of distributed operators running Agent software that participates in 2-of-3 MPC threshold signature consensus to authorize asset movements through a lock-and-mint mechanism. Users interact via the app.hyperunit.xyz frontend, generating Guardian-verified deposit and withdrawal addresses tied to their Hyperliquid accounts. No protocol fees are charged; standard network fees apply.
